_device_comment: device-adapt-1: 'auto' probes the host (cores/RAM/GPU/mobile) at launch via tools.recommend_profile and applies the matching device_profile, so an unconfigured low-spec box stops silently running the heavy 'desktop' tier. The choice is logged at startup and overridable with --device <name> or a config.local.json override. Auto-selection is provably cloud-safe: every profile keeps local_only/cloud-off (tests/test_device_profile_invariants.py).
_warm_on_start_comment: Pre-warm the LLM tiers at startup (core/runtime.py) so turn 1 doesn't pay the ~3s model cold-load on the user's first utterance (measured ttft 3.17s cold vs ~0.27s warm). Set false to measure cold start.
_screen_capture_comment: OFF by default. When enabled, a background loop (core/screen_capture.py) grabs the screen every interval_sec, encodes a frame, and feeds it to the model as AMBIENT visual context via runtime.set_current_frame -- so the assistant sees what you're doing. Needs `pip install mss` (and Pillow to downscale+JPEG; without it sends a full-res PNG). PRIVACY: this continuously reads your screen while on; an image-bearing turn is forced to the on-device/main tier and treated as PRIVATE (never a public cloud chain, Â§9.7). monitor: mss index (1=primary, 0=all). max_width: downscale longest edge (0=full). format: jpeg|png.
_visual_memory_comment: Persistent visual memory (core/visual_memory.py): when memorize is true (AND enabled above), each new frame is turned -- OFF THE HOT PATH -- into a short LOCAL multimodal caption + an OCR snippet and stored as a recallable 'vision' memory (role='observation', source='vision'), retrieved via the same token-budgeted smart-recall layer. PRIVACY (§9.7): caption uses the local model only; recalled vision floats the turn PRIVATE; purge via MemoryManager.clear_observations(). memorize_min_interval_sec throttles the (slow) captioning; memorize_on_change skips a byte-identical frame. OCR needs pytesseract+Pillow+tesseract (degrades to caption-only if absent). Default OFF -- a default run persists nothing.
_gui_actions_comment: Computer-use, READ-ONLY tier (default OFF, separate opt-in from --agent). When enabled (config or --gui-actions), registers screen.identify -- a read-only capability that captures the screen, OCRs/locates UI elements, and reports what is on screen + where (e.g. 'where is the Save button'). It NEVER moves the cursor, clicks, or types. On-screen text is treated as UNTRUSTED (PII-redacted + fenced + Origin.SCREEN) so it can never originate an action. The ACTUATING tier (click/type/press) is intentionally NOT here: it requires owner speaker-ID verification + the always_on_agent/origin action chokepoint + live validation, and is a separate gated slice. Needs mss (capture) + pytesseract/Pillow/tesseract (OCR); degrades gracefully if absent.
_watch_comment: Default OFF + DEFAULT-DENY. The watch/monitor capability lets the owner have the assistant watch a SPECIFIC granted application window for a described event (observe + speak only -- never actuates). Grants (the per-app allowlist) live ONLY in machine-local config.local.json (gitignored) so they never travel between machines or leak which apps the owner watches; this committed file ships an empty allowlist. Granting and starting a watch are owner-verified actions (speaker-ID, via always_on_agent.origin) and are never exposed to the LLM planner. Capture is scoped to the granted window (never full-screen) and X11-only in v1 (Wayland/headless/macOS/Windows degrade to unavailable). min_poll_sec floors the poll cadence; max_active caps simultaneous watches.

llm._router_model_comment: P3 (optional, default unset -> uses fast_model): a dedicated LOCAL model for the CAPABILITY-ROUTER's one-word ACT/SIMPLE/RESEARCH disambiguator slot. Generic small instruct models are weak at it; a function-calling/instruction-tuned small model (e.g. a pulled xLAM-2-3b or Qwen3-4B) does it more reliably. Stays on-device (sees the raw query, §9.7) -- never cloud-wrapped. NOTE: affects only the router's disambiguator; the ReAct planner's tool calls run on the MAIN tier (a multi-step reasoner wants the bigger model). Owner: `ollama pull <model>` then set router_model. Ollama backend only.
llm._think_comment: false (default): skip a reasoning model's silent chain-of-thought on the voice path. gemma4 streams 'thinking' into a separate field BEFORE any spoken content; measured ~9s of dead air before the first word of a story on gemma4:12b (vs ~1.9s with think off). Set true to opt back in (e.g. for a deliberate, non-real-time research tier).

router._comment: threshold=0.3 (was 0.5): combined with the complexity + generation/long-form markers in core/routing.py, this lets reasoning and 'tell me a story' turns escalate from the fast tier to the main model instead of every turn answering on the small model (the field default scored 125/125 turns fast-only). Per-profile overrides (device_profiles.*.llm.router.threshold) still win. See docs/archive/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

llm._live_routing_comment: smart-routing-2 + load follow-up + dynamic hedge (Task 4). OFF by default so behaviour is byte-identical. When true, the runtime feeds the router an ADDITIVE-ONLY, clamped live nudge toward main/cloud from (a) the recorder's rolling local TTFT EWMA and (b) a cheap core.sysinfo SystemMonitor load snapshot (reads the last background sample, no hot-path sampling), and -- for a cloud-racing HedgeLLM -- shortens the per-call hedge_delay_ms (clamped to a floor) so the cloud race starts sooner when local is slow/loaded. A missing/garbage signal is a no-op, so the local tier is never starved. Also honoured via SPEAKER_LIVE_ROUTING=1.

cloud._comment: Single-cloud back-compat: when cloud_providers/cloud_chains are absent, this block configures one cloud client. Set enabled=true and pick a base_url/model/api_key_env. For multi-provider sensitivity-routed chains, use llm.cloud_providers + llm.cloud_chains below and leave this block disabled. allow_prc gates the PRC-hosted (host=CN) presets -- off by default so US-hosted chains are the default; set true to opt in (Decision 2). timeout_s is a short cloud socket timeout (BR1) that reaps a losing worker still blocked in its first-token read fast. max_tokens is an optional per-turn output ceiling (BR4; null = unbounded, composed under each profile's cap). cost_order (smart-routing-5) is OFF by default; set true to stably reorder each cloud_chain's presets by host/ttft/$-per-Mtok metadata (fail-safe: same multiset, original order on any malformed input). Ordering is fastest-then-cheapest WITHIN a host (jurisdiction) tier, never across it: host_rank is the OUTERMOST key, so every US/unknown-hosted preset races before any PRC-hosted (host=CN) one even when the CN preset is cheaper/faster; only inside a host tier does lower ttft (then lower input, then output $-per-Mtok) win.

cloud_providers._comment: Named OpenAI-compatible cloud LLM presets verified 2026-05-28. Each entry: base_url + model + api_key_env + profile (selects per-provider quirk handler in core/llm.py::PROVIDER_PROFILES) + pricing metadata. Selected from a chain in llm.cloud_chains.

openrouter_gpt_oss_120b._comment: OpenRouter (one key, many models; US-hosted aggregator). host=US so it survives the PRC opt-in gate by default (Decision 2). profile openai_compat is the safe generic shape.

cloud_chains._comment: Ordered failover chains by data-sensitivity. HedgeLLM tries the first cloud; on error/timeout it advances to the next; finally falls back to the local main LLM. ALL default chains are US-hosted -- a CN-hosted preset (deepseek/moonshot) is NEVER in a default chain, so even with allow_prc=true a mis-classified personal turn cannot cross to a PRC endpoint by default; add a CN preset explicitly + set allow_prc to opt in. (Defense-in-depth: redact_pii_outbound scrubs high-confidence PII on every outbound cloud prompt regardless of chain.)

cloud_routing._comment: Maps Sensitivity tag (from core/sensitivity.py) -> chain name above. default_chain is used when sensitivity is missing/unknown -- 'private' is the safe default.

desktop._comment: router.threshold=0.3 + the generation/long-form markers (core/routing.py) so reasoning + 'tell me a story' turns escalate to gemma3:12b (fast on this GPU, previously never used). num_predict=768 gives long-form answers room (a story isn't 384 tokens). asr_num_threads=2: a small streaming zipformer doesn't speed up past 2 CPU threads. input_gate ON (core/addressing.py): the fast tier has headroom here, so gate ambient/read-aloud speech instead of answering disfluency fragments. cleanup left OFF -- it adds a second fast-LLM call on the capture thread per final (see docs/archive/perf_audit_2026-05_goal_alignment.md H8). See docs/archive/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

desktop_gpu_4090._comment: RTX 4090 / 5090 / equivalent: gemma3:12b on GPU + gemma3:4b fast tier; input_gate + cleanup + the unified capability_router all ON (fast tier has plenty of headroom). The capability_router mirrors base 'desktop' (was missing here, so the most-capable profile had LESS routing intelligence than the weaker base): action classification CONTROL/SIMPLE/RESEARCH/ACT drives tier+escalate, with fast-LLM disambiguation only on LOW-confidence long turns (set llm_assist=false to drop that extra call if latency matters). Specsim quick TTFA 0.96s. Cloud chains off by default -- local 12b is fast enough; enable in config.local.json for paragraph-length research-mode answers.

macbook_m_series._comment: Apple Silicon (M2/M3/M4 + 16GB+ unified RAM): gemma3:4b main on Metal + gemma3:1b fast. Both gates ON. Specsim quick TTFA 1.46s. Sensitivity-routed cloud chains (hedge strategy): private turns -> Cerebras+Groq (US); code turns -> Cerebras Coder+Kimi; public turns -> OpenRouter+Cerebras (US-only, no CN by default). Provide the US-provider API keys via env to activate; missing keys disable that chain individually. (A CN preset is opt-in only: add it to a chain AND set allow_prc.)

cloud._consent: SECURITY: default OFF -- cloud must be enabled DELIBERATELY (set enabled=true), never silently on API-key presence. When on, only post-ASR text crosses (US-only chains by default) + redact_pii_outbound scrubs PII.

cpu_laptop._comment: Mid-range Windows/Linux laptop, no dedicated GPU (8 cores, 16GB). Local gemma3:1b only -- the 4b main is too slow on pure CPU (specsim quick TTFA would be ~3.5s+). Sensitivity-routed cloud chains AVAILABLE (hedge_delay_ms=0 = kick cloud immediately) but DEFAULT OFF (cloud.enabled=false) -- enable deliberately; on CPU the cloud is the practical answer tier, but it must be an explicit opt-in, not silent on key-presence. Both gates OFF (no fast-tier headroom on CPU).

cloud._consent: SECURITY: default OFF -- enable deliberately. hedge_delay_ms=0 would hit cloud on EVERY turn, so silent activation on key-presence is especially undesirable here.

open_speaker._comment: EXPLICIT (non-auto) profile for open-speaker barge-in WITHOUT headphones (the owner HARD REQUIREMENT). Select with `--device open_speaker`. Turns on the production WebRTC AudioProcessingModule (AEC3 + residual-echo suppressor + ML noise suppression + AGC2 + high-pass filter) for the capture path -- it tolerates the NONLINEAR open laptop speaker where the linear NLMS filter measures ~0 dB ERLE and diverges. Needs `pip install livekit` (build_aec fails OPEN to no-AEC if absent) -- it exposes rtc.AudioProcessingModule and runs standalone (no room). Inherits everything else from the base profile; layers gemma3:4b/1b like cpu_laptop so it runs on a real laptop. apm_always_on runs the APM on every block so its NS/AGC also clean the user's own idle utterance (the desktop analog of the OS voice-comm path the Android app already uses). This makes the working open-speaker config reproducible from a CLEAN CLONE instead of living only in a machine-local config.local.json. Re-enroll your voice after enabling (the speaker embedding shifts post-cleanup). VALIDATE live at the mic (barge_stress) before trusting it.

sherpa._capture_voice_comm_note: Set capture_voice_comm=true on Windows (WASAPI Communications). On Linux load PipeWire module-echo-cancel and point input_device at the EC source instead (see docs/audio_pipeline.md).

phone._comment: router.threshold=0.55 overrides the base 0.3: on phone the 'main' tier is gemma-3-4b GGUF on CPU (n_gpu_layers=0), so over-escalating idiom/borderline turns onto it is a real latency hit. Keep escalation reserved for clearly heavy turns here (the doc-prescribed phone safety valve, docs/archive/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md).

llm._kv_cache_comment: llm-inference-9: q8_0 on the K cache (V left at f16) ~halves the K-cache memory at near-lossless quality WITHOUT needing flash-attention (V-cache quant does, which isn't validated on this hardware). Validate on-device before trusting absolutes.

options._num_predict_comment: llm-inference-3: cap on-device output. LlamaCppLLM translates num_predict -> llama.cpp's max_tokens; without it the cap was ignored and the model generated to n_ctx on CPU. A voice reply rarely needs more than this.

phone_lite._comment: Low-end phone (6GB RAM, mid-range SoC): single-tier gemma3:1b GGUF Q4 only. The 4b model thrashes RAM at this tier. Cloud chains AVAILABLE with fallback strategy (cloud-first w/ ttft_deadline, local-only last resort) but DEFAULT OFF (cloud.enabled=false) -- enable deliberately, never silently on key-presence. Specsim quick TTFA on-device 5s; with cloud 1-2s. Gates OFF.

options._num_predict_comment: llm-inference-3: cap on-device output (translated to llama.cpp max_tokens). Tightest tier -> tightest cap.

cloud._consent: SECURITY: default OFF -- enable deliberately, never silently on API-key presence.

sherpa._asr_comment: asr-tts-1: per-tier ASR policy. On the weakest tier drop the offline second pass (streaming finals only) and use greedy decode -- the offline SenseVoice re-transcribe + beam search are desktop luxuries that cost real-time headroom on a mid-range SoC, where the streaming zipformer final is good enough. Desktop/4090 keep beam + SenseVoice (base defaults).

input_gate._comment: Optional ACT/INGEST gate (core/addressing.py). Disabled by default; enable to stop the assistant from answering ambient speech / read-aloud text. Needs llm.fast_model. unsure_acts: OWNER DECISION 2026-06-10 ('speak only with good confidence the input is addressed to her') -- the enabled device profiles ship unsure_acts=false (UNSURE stays quiet; an occasional missed borderline ask beats answering house noise). The classifier prompt also INGESTs recognizer noise / word salad explicitly now.

cleanup._comment: Optional ASR transcript cleanup (core/cleanup.py). Disabled by default; enable to rewrite the raw final into the user's intended sentence -- drops fillers, resolves self-corrections, handles the word-repeat-at-end correction signal. Needs llm.fast_model. Adds one fast-tier LLM call per ACT'd final.

capability_router._comment: Unified capability router (core/capability_router.py) -- the 'middle layer' that decides, ONCE per turn, whether the turn is CONTROL (stop/mode switch), SIMPLE (one-shot reply), RESEARCH (gather/multi-step via the ReAct planner), or ACT (perform an action) + the fast/main tier. Disabled by default so behaviour is byte-identical (the existing per-gate routing stands). When enabled it BACKS both the tier Router (core/routing.py) and the planner escalate decision (always_on_agent/react.py) from one coherent module, reusing should_escalate + the tier heuristic so it never diverges from what the brain would have done. llm_assist=true uses the fast tier to disambiguate ONLY low-confidence turns (needs llm.fast_model; memoized so it costs at most one fast-LLM call per turn); confidence_threshold is the heuristic confidence below which the LLM is consulted. Device-adaptive: a weak profile sets llm_assist=false (or omits the fast model) and pays nothing. act_confirm is reserved -- it gates STATE-CHANGING action tools behind a spoken confirmation once such tools are registered (v1 routes ACT to the planner's read-only tools, so it is a no-op today).

continuation._comment: ADD-ON / continuation: when a follow-up extends the in-flight assistant turn ('and also...', 'oh wait make it shorter', 'what about Mars too'), merge it into one coherent answer instead of racing a competing cold task. Before the turn starts speaking the two prompts are merged into one fresh turn; after it's already speaking the add-on is answered as a context-carrying continuation queued behind it (no re-speak, no overlap). Deterministic + local (no LLM on this path). markers/trailing_markers/modifier_leads override the built-in EN/RO cue sets; addon_max_words bounds the short-modifier branch.

resume._comment: Resume-after-interrupt + L4 self-echo guard (2026-06-10 owner feedback; core/resume.py). (a) 'start again'/'continue'/'go on' (EN+RO) after a barge/stop CUT becomes a continue-from-where-you-stopped prompt embedding the last spoken_tail_chars of what was actually SPOKEN -- a stopped story resumes, it does not restart or greet. Only fires when the prior reply was genuinely cut; otherwise the phrase passes through unchanged. (b) echo_guard: a final arriving within echo_window_sec of playback end whose tokens overlap the just-spoken sentences >= echo_min_overlap is the assistant's own TTS echo (live: it answered its own 'How can I help you today?' at high speaker volume) -> dropped before addressing/ingest. Volume-independent (pure text), unlike the L1-L3 energy floors.
resume._echo_window_comment: Window covers the FINAL's arrival, not the echo audio: the recognizer adds endpoint silence (~1-2s) + utterance length before the final fires (live: the verbatim echo final landed ~4s after playback end and slipped a 3s window). Short garbled tails ('Leleep.' from 'spin and leap') are caught by char-fuzzy matching vs the last sentence's tail; confirm/deny words are exempt from the fuzzy rule.

turn_merge._comment: Hold-and-merge final dispatch (2026-06-10; core/turn_merge.py). A final that READS mid-thought -- ends on a conjunction/article/preposition ('A long story about') or is a 1-2 word fragment ('So they') -- is HELD up to hold_sec for the user's next words and MERGED into one query, instead of each fragment being answered (the live failure: four fragments, four spoken replies, each reply's echo then destabilized barge-in). Complete-reading finals dispatch with NO added latency; control words (yes/no/stop/never mind/...) are exempt. Partials extend an open hold (cap max_hold_sec). Also moves ALL final processing (addressing gate -> cleaner -> router) off the audio capture thread onto a dispatch worker (review rc-2: LLM calls were blocking mic reads). Complements 'continuation' above, which only merges while a task is still in flight (~0.6s on the fast tier).

assistant._comment: Optional persona so the model knows WHAT IT IS. Its SKILLS are enumerated automatically from the live capability manifest (always_on_agent/capabilities.py specs) into the system prompt, and the web-access line reflects the real web_search.enabled / Â§9.7 egress state. Leave name empty for the anonymous 'local voice assistant' identity; set name (and optionally a one-line persona / extra guidance) to give it a character.

planner._comment: enabled=true makes the ReAct planner reachable: ASSISTANT-mode queries that hit an _ESCALATE_MARKER (search/find/compare/'and then'/step by step -- see always_on_agent/react.py) run a bounded plan->execute loop over the tools below instead of degrading to a one-shot reply. Gated, so ordinary short turns stay one-shot. web.search returns corpus-only results until web_search.enabled=true (egress-gated, Â§9.7). See docs/archive/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

web_search._comment: FLAT web.search config (P3, Decision 3). Disabled by default: the runtime ships corpus-only (search.local). To enable real web research, set enabled=true and base_url to your self-hosted SearXNG (keyless). Every query is routed through the Â§9.7 egress gate (core.sensitivity.may_leave_device) BEFORE any network call -- PII/personal queries never egress (corpus only). timeout_s bounds the connect+read GET (BR7; the only wedge bound since a blocking GET can't poll cancel). max_results caps mapped hits.

sherpa._asr_final_comment: TWO-pass ASR (DEFAULT-ON when the model is present): the streaming transducer above gives low-latency partials + the endpoint; the endpointed UTTERANCE is then RE-transcribed by the stronger OFFLINE SenseVoice (sees the whole utterance) for the FINAL text that reaches the LLM. BAKEOFF + LIVE 2026-06-01 on real voice: the streaming zipformer garbles run-on/casual speech ('HEY IRIC LISTENING TO ME', 'THIS TURNED OFF THE KITCHEN LIGHT') while SenseVoice nails it clean+punctuated ('Hey, are you listening to me.', 'Please turn off the kitchen light.') and the user confirmed it 'felt nicer'. MEASURED second-pass cost on real utterances: ~55ms median (2 threads) -- a small, worthwhile trade. Accurate, robust, reliable (Moonshine returned empty on half the clips; Whisper ~2x slower). SenseVoice also brings punctuation+casing+ITN, so _postprocess (casing/punct models) is skipped on the second-pass final. The model path is the STANDARD setup_models location: present -> two-pass active; ABSENT -> build_final_recognizer returns None -> streaming-only (BYTE-IDENTICAL). Get it: python -m tools.setup_models --sense-voice. Set asr_final_backend='' to force streaming-only. asr_final_min_sec skips the pass on tiny utterances the streaming model already nails.
sherpa._asr_final_language_comment: PIN the SenseVoice language. 'en' forces English (REQUIRED: SenseVoice is multilingual and its auto-detect mis-fired to Chinese on short English utterances -- 'HEY HEY'->'å˜¿å˜º', 'HOW ARE YOU'->'å‘¦'). Change to 'zh'/'ja'/'ko'/'yue', or '' for auto-detect, to test other languages.
sherpa._asr_hotwords_comment: Newline-separated bias phrases for the STREAMING transducer only. With asr_final_backend='sense_voice' ON (the default), the second pass overrides the streaming text for normal-length turns, so a name fixed here will NOT reach the LLM-facing final -- use asr_final_hr_dict_dir/asr_final_hr_lexicon/asr_final_hr_rule_fsts (SenseVoice homophone replacement) to bias the FINAL. See docs/asr_biasing.md.
sherpa._endpoint_comment: Semantic (turn-completion) endpointing layered on the acoustic rule2 timer (core/endpointing.py). A turn-completion detector + adaptive policy commit a final EARLY when the partial reads as a complete turn (down to endpoint_min_silence_sec) and HOLD past rule2 (up to endpoint_max_silence_sec) when it ends mid-phrase ('...and', '...the'). ENABLED 2026-05-30 after an on-device A/B (docs/archive/live_validation_run_2026-05-30.md): it reclaims the dominant ~1.2s trailing-silence wait -> ~0.9s endpoint, ~300ms first-audio win, with NO tail clipping and NO sentence splitting across all scenarios. endpoint_min_silence_sec=0.7 (raised from 0.5) is the validated floor: it must exceed BOTH the decoder lookahead (or an early commit clips the last word) AND a typical intra-sentence comma pause (at 0.5 a run-on like 'Hey, what are you, and...' split at the comma; 0.7 fixed the split while keeping the win). Re-validate per deployment with `python -m tools.live_session --all --inject --smart-endpoint` and diff the ON finals against the acoustic (OFF) finals before trusting a new value/model. The shipped detector is lexical; a Smart Turn v3 audio (prosody) model drops in via the same seam.
sherpa._endpoint_high_confidence_comment: Adaptive confidence-tiered SHORTEN floor (2026-06-01). A HIGH-confidence completion (lexical 0.75 bin -- a normal ending word, NEVER a conjunction/article/filler) commits at endpoint_high_confidence_floor (0.6s) instead of the full endpoint_min_silence_sec (0.7s), reclaiming ~110ms endpoint p50 / first-audio on the common well-formed-turn case. VALIDATED on-device 2026-06-01: floor=0.6 cut endpoint p50 918->806ms with text-diffs EQUAL to the inject run-to-run variance floor (6 vs 6 -- no extra splits/truncations) and the 'In one sentence, how do plants...' comma run-on stayed INTACT; floor=0.55 truncated it (13 diffs, 2x variance) and was rejected. 0.6 still exceeds the decoder lookahead AND a typical comma pause. A medium-confidence complete keeps the full 0.7 floor. Set endpoint_high_confidence_floor=0 to revert to the uniform floor. A premature commit is merged back by the continuation layer. Re-validate with `python -m tools.live_session --scenario latency_profile_mixed --scenario self_awareness_enumerate_do_decline --inject --endpoint-high-confidence-floor 0.6` and diff the finals vs a floor-0 run.
sherpa._endpoint_detector_comment: Turn-completion detector: 'lexical' (default, text-only, cheap) or 'prosody' (Smart Turn v3 audio model -- reads the rising/sustained intonation of a mid-thought trailing-off that lexical can't, so the floor can drop further). Prosody needs endpoint_prosody_model (download: python -m tools.setup_models --turn-model). The completion_score becomes the model's P(complete), so the SAME adaptive policy applies (>=0.75 -> high_confidence_floor; <=0.3 -> EXTEND/hold). VALIDATED on the user's REAL voice 2026-06-01 (tools/turn_detect_check): complete 0.74-0.98 vs incomplete 0.01-0.56, margin 0.18 -- but the model is HUMAN-AUDIO ONLY (flat ~0.97 on TTS), so it CANNOT be validated with the synthetic-user/inject harness; validate the live floor-lowering on real speech (python -m core --engine sherpa, then read logs/runs/*.summary.json endpoint latency + transcripts). endpoint_prosody_min_silence gates the ~15ms model to the trailing-silence window (not every block). Default detector stays 'lexical' (byte-identical) until the live floor-lowering is validated.
sherpa._denoise_comment: Optional speech denoiser (sherpa-onnx GTCRN) on the 16 kHz capture block, applied right after resampling and BEFORE the recognizer, the speaker embedder, and the VAD all read it. Broadband noise both garbles STT and drags the enrolled-user speaker embedding below the gate (0.30-0.46 vs the 0.5 threshold -> lockout); one denoise cleans the single block for every downstream model. OFF by default: denoise_enabled=false OR denoise_model='' builds NOTHING and the capture path is byte-identical to no-denoise (gate mirrors endpoint_enabled). Fetch the 523KB model with `python -m tools.setup_models --denoise-model` (wires denoise_model into config.local.json), then set denoise_enabled=true. Costs one tiny ONNX inference per 0.1s block on the capture thread (GTCRN RTF<<1; measure with tools.noise_stress before enabling on weak tiers). NOTE: the recorder writes the DENOISED block, so a recording made with denoise ON replays already-denoised -- keep this flag consistent across record/replay or you double-denoise. Re-enroll your voice after enabling (the embedding shifts vs an un-denoised enrollment).
sherpa._aec_comment: On-device Acoustic Echo Cancellation (core/engines/_aec.py) -- subtracts the assistant's own played TTS (far-end) from the mic block so the loop does NOT self-interrupt on open speakers (the no-AEC failure). Runs after resample, BEFORE denoise/VAD/recognizer/speaker-gate. OFF by default (aec_enabled=false -> byte-identical capture path). When ON, the barge-in gate uses aec_relaxed_margin_db (echo already cancelled -> soft interrupts audible again) instead of barge_in_output_margin_db. backend: 'nlms' = dependency-free NumPy adaptive filter (ships now, ~10-20 dB real-world ERLE, no extra deps); 'dtln' = deep ONNX tier (deferred, currently no-op). aec_ref_delay_ms: speaker->mic delay for the far-end reference -- CALIBRATE per device with tools/echo_probe.py (a wrong value degrades cancellation). aec_filter_taps: modeled echo tail (rounded to a power of two; also the added capture latency in samples -- 512 ~= 32 ms). aec_doubletalk_freeze: stop the filter diverging onto the user's voice during talk-over. VALIDATE on device (echo_probe ERLE + a live barge-in run) BEFORE setting aec_enabled=true.
sherpa._tts_target_rms_comment: Per-sentence TTS loudness normalization (core/audio_frontend.normalize_rms). The offline VITS voice emits a DIFFERENT absolute amplitude per sentence (a function of phonetics/length: measured RMS 0.108-0.130, peak 0.726-0.863 -> a >2 dB sentence-to-sentence swing the listener hears as uneven/choppy 'not fluid' delivery, and a swinging open-speaker echo floor the barge gate can't settle on). 0.12 normalizes every sentence to a steady ~speech RMS (soft-knee limited on peaks). Tradeoff: switches synthesis from the streaming callback to whole-clip synth (needs the full clip to measure RMS) -> ~0.1s more first-audio latency. 0.0 = OFF (streaming path, byte-identical).
sherpa._input_loudness_comment: Optional LOUDNESS gate (near-field user signal, layered on voice-identity). The user is CLOSE to the mic (loud); a TV/far speaker sits near the ambient floor. When >0, a final whose voice-identity DIPPED is still admitted if its level is >= this many dB above the running ambient-noise floor -- so a wavering embedding never wrongly drops the user (a rescue; never rejects what identity accepted). 0 = identity-only. Try ~10-15 dB. The ambient floor is an asymmetric EWMA (falls fast, rises slow) tracked only when this is >0.
sherpa._final_floor_comment: L1 echo gate on the FINAL-dispatch path. A completed ASR final reaches the brain only when its level is >= this many dB above the device's LEARNED echo/quiet floor = max(_ambient_rms quiet floor, _playback_floor_rms playback residual-echo floor), both learned online. The assistant's own open-speaker echo / ambient noise that the recognizer turned into words ('BEING', 'THIRTEEN') sits AT that floor and is dropped; real speech is many dB above and passes. RELATIVE (dB above the learned per-device floor), never an absolute RMS -- so it auto-adapts to the machine+room. This is the root fix for the self-interrupt echo-final cascade (run-20260608-181250). Fail-open until a floor is learned. 0 disables. ~6 dB is conservative/admit-leaning; raise if echo finals still slip, lower if quiet real speech is dropped. DEPENDENCY: a learned floor exists only when aec_enabled=true (playback echo floor) OR input_loudness_margin_db>0 (quiet floor); with NEITHER the gate is inert/fail-open (the engine logs a warning). The open-speaker configs that cascade run AEC, so the gate is live there; on an echo-free setup it harmlessly abstains.
sherpa._barge_in_refractory_comment: L2 post-speaking refractory: for this long after the assistant stops speaking (a turn ends or a barge cancels it), suppress a RE-fired barge-in so the just-cancelled utterance's echo TAIL can't immediately self-interrupt the next reply. A fixed clearance window (a time, not an energy threshold); 0 disables.

memory._comment: FLAT P2 memory seam config (R10) -- single-level so a device-profile override survives the shallow merge. backend: auto|inmemory|postgres|sqlite (auto picks Postgres only when $DATABASE_URL is set; sqlite = the persistent on-device store at sqlite_path, default ~/.speaker/memory.db, for desktop-without-Postgres continuity across restarts -- same protocol + recall as in-RAM, rows just survive). recall_enabled is OFF by default: recall must be measured (tools.bench) before enabling. P2b (live, Postgres-only): profile_enabled gates the default-OFF user-profile producer (deterministic regex at ingest + optional fast-LLM pass on the writer thread); episodic_ttl_days/summary_ttl_days are the age TTLs apply_retention() enforces at close-time -- episodic 'messages' are summarized-then-evicted past episodic_ttl_days, 'summaries' dropped past summary_ttl_days, user_profile never TTL'd. The rolling summary uses the fast LLM off the bus thread.
memory._recall_budget_comment: Recall is now bounded by ONE token budget + a data-derived adaptive cutoff (always_on_agent/recall.py), NOT fixed char caps/thresholds. recall_max_tokens caps the whole injected recall block (header included); chars_per_token is the per-device token-estimate ratio (a measurement knob, device-profile tunable, NOT a relevance threshold). recall_cutoff_k / recall_dedup_ratio default to 0 = fully data-derived (the no-magic-number device-adaptive path); a positive value is an opt-in escape hatch (cutoff_k also requires score>=mean+k*std; dedup_ratio pins a SequenceMatcher ratio). recall_recent_reserve_tokens (0=off) folds the recent-conversation block under a token cap so recall+recent can't independently stack. recall_max_chars is a DEPRECATED legacy alias (derives max_tokens=chars//4) kept one release; recall_min_similarity + recall_max_items were dead no-ops (zero readers) and are removed.
memory._recall_scoring_comment: Multi-signal recall scoring (Generative-Agents: relevance + recency + importance), all default-OFF (weights 0 = relevance-only, byte-identical). recall_recency_weight>0 scales each recalled item by an exponential recency-decay (recall_recency_half_life_days = the age in days at which the factor halves) so a fresh salient remark outranks a stale one; recall_importance_weight>0 boosts durable kinds (profile>summary>message>vision). Multiplicative on the native score so the adaptive cutoff stays scale-invariant. NOTE: recency-ranked recall is single-backend by design (backends stamp different wall-clocks for the same logical add).
memory._recent_context_comment: Short-term conversation memory: inject the last recent_context_turns user/assistant turns into the answering model's prompt so it can resolve references ('its', 'the second one', 'make it shorter') to what was just said. Cheap (no embeddings, unlike recall_* above which surfaces relevant PAST-session snippets). Default ON; bounded by turns + chars (+ recall_recent_reserve_tokens when >0).
memory._recent_context_reset_comment: Topic reset (2026-06-10 plan step 5): a short user utterance matching a reset phrase ('start again', 'never mind', 'new topic', ...) suppresses the recent-conversation block for its own turn AND cuts the block off there for later turns, so an abandoned topic can't bleed back in (live failure: after 'Start again' the model re-apologized about the stale volume topic). reset_max_words bounds matching to short utterances; phrases overridable via recent_context_reset_phrases.
memory._procedural_comment: Procedural memory (default OFF): when on, an explicit teach directive in user speech ('from now on...', 'always...', 'never...', 'call me X') is captured as a durable behavior RULE (stored never-TTL'd) and injected as a small TRUSTED 'Standing user preferences' instruction block on every turn, so the assistant follows it going forward. Default OFF keeps the prompt byte-identical until opted in.
memory._continuity_comment: cross_session_continuity (lm-2, default OFF like recall_enabled, Postgres tier): on a fresh process seed the rolling-summary head from the newest persisted summary AND fall back to the most-recent user messages ACROSS sessions (the session_id regenerates each run), so memory survives a restart. Default OFF keeps the opening-turn context byte-identical until measured/opted-in. The SQLite backend is inherently persistent so it doesn't need this.
memory._persist_assistant_comment: persist_assistant (lm-5, default OFF, Postgres tier): persist the assistant's finals as recallable rows (role='assistant', off the bus thread) AND admit them to search_memory, so the Postgres tier matches the in-RAM/SQLite backends, which already store + recall assistant_output items. Default OFF keeps recall output byte-identical (no assistant rows are written OR read). lm-3 (the §9.7 sensitivity float over the recall block) is the precondition and is already in place.

agent_brain._allowlist_comment: AUTO-SAFE (no-confirm) commands ONLY. SECURITY: this tier must contain NOTHING that can execute arbitrary code, read arbitrary FILE CONTENT (exfiltration), or write/modify. So no `python -c`/interpreter inline-eval, no cat/head/tail (arbitrary file read), no echo (redirect). Only no-effect status commands. Defense-in-depth: core/agent.py _NEVER_AUTO_SAFE downgrades any code-exec/chaining/redirect/newline to needs-confirm even if a pattern here matches, and require_owner_verified gates the whole capability on owner-verified live audio.
